title: Threshold sweep: optimal default from 85 to 75
description: Swept --threshold values across 16 example logs to find optimal default. The original 85 was a magic number with no empirical basis.
design:
## Methodology

Swept 9 threshold values (50, 60, 70, 75, 80, 85, 90, 95, 100) across 16
example log files, then narrowed to 72-78 range with quality spot-checks.

### Test corpus (16 files)
apache (100), apiserver (4.8k), argocd-controller (28k), argocd-server (61k),
cilium (38k), harbor-postgres-primary (54k), harbor-postgres-replica (24k),
hdfs (100), kubelet (71k), microservices (53), nginx (50), prometheus (100),
rancher (22k), realistic-repetitive (70), security-attacks (64), vllm (3.2k)

## Results

### Wide sweep (avg across 16 files)
| Threshold | Avg Compression | Avg Output Lines |
|-----------|----------------|-----------------|
| 50% | 88.5% | 52 |
| 60% | 87.6% | 87 |
| 70% | 82.0% | 178 |
| 75% | 80.3% | 197 |
| 80% | 79.9% | 215 |
| 85% | 77.8% | 232 |
| 90% | 75.0% | 252 |
| 100% | 71.5% | 351 |

### Narrow sweep (72-78)
| Threshold | Avg Compression | Avg Output |
|-----------|----------------|------------|
| 72% | 82.0% | 181 |
| 73% | 82.0% | 185 |
| 74% | 80.8% | 191 |
| 75% | 80.3% | 197 |
| 76% | 80.1% | 201 |
| 77% | 79.9% | 206 |
| 78% | 80.0% | 209 |

## Quality cliff at 73

At threshold=73, nginx log merges HTTP 304 and 200 responses into one group.
At 74+, they correctly separate. This is the critical boundary — below 74,
different HTTP status codes (different semantic meaning) get merged.

Security log at 73 merges SSH admin+root attempts. At 74+ they stay separate.

## Key findings by log type

- **HDFS**: 85 splits identical "delete block" ops into 5 groups (by block ID).
  75 correctly merges to 1 group.
- **Prometheus**: Same pattern — "Deleting obsolete block" splits at 85, merges at 75.
- **Security**: All attack types stay properly separated at 75. At 50-60, SSH/SQL/rate-limit bleed together.
- **k8s (kubelet, cilium, rancher)**: 1-2% better compression at 75 with no quality loss.
- **nginx**: Correctly separates status codes at 74+, merges them at 73-.

## Decision
Changed default from 85 → 75. The 85 was a magic number from the first commit
with no testing. 75 gives 3% better avg compression with zero quality regressions.
close_reason: Research complete. Default changed 85→75 in commit b80432c.

title: Remove --max-tokens flag
description:
Remove the --max-tokens flag entirely. The fake token counting (words/0.75) is dishonest
and the use case (cap output for LLM context windows) is better served by head -n or
a future --max-output-lines flag.

1. Remove max_tokens field from Cli struct in src/main.rs
2. Remove parse_token_count function from src/main.rs
3. Remove max_tokens from Config struct in src/config.rs
4. Remove all token counting/limiting logic in folder.rs and main.rs
5. Remove count_tokens methods from folder.rs and analyzer.rs
6. Update any tests that reference max_tokens
7. Remove from CLAUDE.md flags list

Acceptance: --max-tokens is not in lessence --help and cargo test passes.
close_reason: Removed --max-tokens flag and all fake token counting

title: Unit tests: remaining pattern detectors (quoted, process, email, uuid)
description:
Add unit tests for remaining pattern detector functions.

1. QuotedStringDetector::detect_and_replace (20 mutants): test single/double quotes, nested quotes, empty strings.
2. ProcessDetector::detect_and_replace (20 mutants): test PID, thread ID detection.
3. EmailDetector functions (various): test valid emails, false positives, edge cases.
4. UuidDetector functions (various): test UUID v4, v5, request IDs, trace IDs.
5. HashDetector functions (various): test MD5, SHA1, SHA256, SHA512 detection.
6. HttpStatusDetector functions (various): test 2xx/3xx/4xx/5xx, false positives.

Lower priority because these have some existing unit tests already (tests/unit/).

title: Create agent skill for lessence
description:
Create .claude/skills/lessence-use/SKILL.md teaching coding agents when/how to use lessence.

1. Same pattern as playwright-cli skill: judgment, not documentation
2. When to use: test failure 500+ lines, kubectl dumps, CI failures, repetitive walls
3. When NOT to: structured JSON (jq), specific known error (grep), small output (<50 lines)
4. Interpret output: [+N similar] = repeating problem, many groups = diverse problems
5. Ships with current feature set, improves as new features land
6. Example invocations for common scenarios (k8s, CI, crash loops)

Acceptance: skill file loads in Claude Code and triggers on relevant contexts.

comments.author: claude
comments.text: Created as user-profile skill at ~/.claude/skills/lessence/SKILL.md instead of project-scoped. Triggers on verbose log scenarios across all projects.

title: Top-N frequency mode (--top N)
description:
Show only the N most frequent patterns, sorted by count descending.

1. New flag: --top N implies sort by frequency desc (no separate --sort)
2. Output: count prominently shown, e.g. "[1247x] Connection refused from <IP>"
3. Footer: "(showing top 5 of 142 patterns, 98.2% of lines)"
4. Data already available from folding (group counts), needs reorder + truncate

Prior art: logmine sorts by cluster size by default. angle-grinder has
composable pipeline. --top N is the simplest UX for a pipe tool.

Acceptance: lessence --top 5 shows exactly 5 groups sorted by count desc.
close_reason: Implemented --top N flag with frequency-sorted output

title: Synthetic log generator for constitutional tests
description:
Completed: tests/fixtures/log_generator.rs generates deterministic
kubelet-style logs with configurable line count. Used by
test_constitutional_compliance_generated which runs in CI without
corpus files.

1. Generator covers: UUID, IP, Hash, Path, PodName, Namespace, PID, Timestamp, Duration, QuotedString
2. 1000 lines = 98.5% compression, 500 lines = 97%
3. Runs in debug and release builds
4. Kubelet.log tests kept as bonus local-only coverage

title: Accept file arguments
description:
Add optional positional file args to clap, fall back to stdin when none given.

1. Add files: Vec<PathBuf> positional arg to Cli struct
2. Support - as explicit stdin (universal convention: cat, rg, bat, jq)
3. Multiple files concatenated into one stream (like cat/jq)
4. Shell handles glob expansion (no built-in glob needed)
5. Error handling: warn and skip bad files, process the rest (like cat)
6. Binary detection: check first 8KB for null bytes, warn and skip

Acceptance: lessence app.log produces same output as lessence < app.log.
Items 1 and 5 in .ideas/features.md are duplicates of this.

title: Unit tests: normalize.rs similarity_score
description:
Add unit tests for Normalizer::similarity_score in src/normalize.rs to kill remaining mutants.

1. similarity_score (32 mutants from quick run, 30 caught, 2 missed): math operators in Levenshtein distance calculation. Pin exact scores for known input pairs. Test symmetric property, self-similarity=100, empty strings, identical strings, completely different strings.
2. normalize_line (8 mutants from quick run): test short-circuit logic, OR operator mutations. Test empty input, single token, multiple tokens.

Proptest already covers commutativity and self-similarity, but unit tests with pinned values would catch operator mutations.

title: Clean up help text (remove LLM references)
description:
Remove LLM positioning from CLI help descriptions.

4 references in src/main.rs:
1. Line 17: strip_ansi_codes doc "for LLM consumption" -> remove
2. Line 98: Preflight "for LLM agents" -> "for automation/CI"
3. Line 122: preserve_color "for LLM optimization" -> remove qualifier
4. Line 126: max_tokens "for LLM limits" -> "limit output size"

Acceptance: lessence --help contains zero mentions of LLM.
close_reason: Removed all 4 LLM references from help text

title: Unit tests: pattern detectors (timestamp, network, path)
description:
Add unit tests for pattern detector functions that have high mutant counts.

1. UnifiedTimestampDetector::has_timestamp_indicators (66 mutants): checks if a string has timestamp-like patterns. Test positive/negative cases.
2. NetworkDetector::detect_and_replace (58 mutants): detects IPs, ports, FQDNs. Test IPv4, IPv6, port ranges, false positives.
3. NetworkDetector::is_plausible_ipv (26 mutants): validates IP-like strings. Test boundary cases.
4. PathDetector::is_likely_file_path (30 mutants): classifies file paths. Test Unix, Windows, relative, false positives.
5. PathDetector::is_likely_url_path (28 mutants): classifies URL paths. Test http/https, false positives.
6. DurationDetector::detect_and_replace (38 mutants): detects time durations. Test ms, s, m, h formats.

Tests go in each pattern module's cfg(test) section or tests/unit/.

title: Investigate cargo-mutants memory blowup
description:
cargo test alone uses 187MB peak. cargo-mutants process uses 430MB.
But when mutants runs cargo test inside a systemd scope, system memory
climbs to 44GB+ and triggers OOM kills.

Known facts:
1. Normal cargo test: 187MB peak, 16s wall time, 335 tests pass
2. cargo-mutants process: 430MB peak (from /usr/bin/time -v)
3. Inside systemd scope: OOM killed at 14.7GB RSS on the test binary
4. --gitignore was false by default, copying 550MB of fuzz artifacts (now fixed)
5. With --gitignore true, copy is ~10MB but memory still blows up
6. 1 job, not parallel
7. Chrome/VS Code use ~10GB normally, system has 62GB total

Unanswered: what inside cargo-mutants' execution causes 14.7GB RSS
on the test binary when the same binary uses 187MB standalone?
Possible leads: mutants' temp dir build cache, incremental compilation
artifacts, multiple test binaries compiled simultaneously, or
something about how cargo test behaves differently in the temp tree.

Reproduce: make mutants (observe memory via htop or /usr/bin/time -v)

title: Unit tests: stats reporting functions
description:
Add unit tests for stats reporting functions in src/folder.rs to kill mutants.

1. count_pattern_types (84 mutants): increments stats counters per Token variant. Test each token type maps to correct counter. Tricky: Port increments ips, Json increments paths, BracketContext/KV/etc increment percentages.
2. count_active_pattern_types (110 mutants): counts non-zero stats fields. Test 0/1/all non-zero. Note: emails field may be unchecked (potential bug).
3. print_stats (104 mutants): writes markdown report to writer. Test with Vec<u8>, assert sections present, pattern rows only when count > 0, compression ratio thresholds.
4. print_stats_json (mutants): JSON to stderr. Test valid JSON, all fields present.
5. print_summary_json (mutants): JSON with type=summary. Test flattened structure, all fields.

All private functions, tests go in cfg(test) mod tests in folder.rs.

title: Unit test: format_group_json stats counting
description:
Missed mutant: src/folder.rs:759 replace && with || in format_group_json.

The condition gates whether collapsed_groups and lines_saved stats are
incremented in JSON mode. Changing && to || goes undetected because no
test asserts on these stats fields in JSON output specifically.

Fix: add a unit test that processes lines through JSON mode and verifies
collapsed_groups and lines_saved match expected values. Test both with
and without essence_mode to exercise both branches of the condition.

Part of lessence-okn (format_group unit tests).

title: Research: line truncation in CLI summary output
description: Researched how CLI tools handle long lines in output. Findings inform whether lessence --summary should truncate.
design:
## Question
Should lessence --summary truncate long representative lines? Currently
hardcoded at 200 chars with "...". No research was done before picking 200.

## How Other Tools Handle It

### Search tools
- **ripgrep**: No truncation by default. Opt-in via --max-columns N (omits line)
  or --max-columns N + --max-columns-preview (truncates with prefix). Recommended
  config in GUIDE.md: --max-columns=150.
- **ag (silver searcher)**: --width NUM truncates. No default. Separately skips
  lines >2k chars by default.
- **bat**: Auto-wraps to terminal width by default. --chop-long-lines to truncate
  instead of wrap. --terminal-width for explicit override.
- **ack**: No truncation at all.

### Log tools
- **lnav**: Most sophisticated — per-field max_width with overflow algorithms:
  truncate, dot-dot (middle ellipsis), abbrev (com.example → c.e), last-word.
  Config-based, not CLI flag.
- **angle-grinder**: Adapts to terminal width, no explicit flag.
- **logmine, drain3**: No truncation.

### System tools
- **docker ps**: Hardcoded per-column: COMMAND=20 chars, ID=12 chars. --no-trunc
  to disable. Does NOT detect terminal width.
- **kubectl get**: No truncation at all. Infamously wide (k8s issue #7843).
- **git log --oneline**: No truncation. Full first line regardless of length.
- **ps**: Truncates COMMAND to terminal width. -ww for unlimited.

### Terminal width detection
- Tools use ioctl(TIOCGWINSZ) or tput cols. $COLUMNS is shell-local, not
  inherited. Non-TTY (pipes) can't detect width — fall back to 80 or unlimited.

## Findings
1. No tool hardcodes 200 — arbitrary number with no precedent.
2. The dominant pattern: no truncation by default, opt-in flag for users who want it.
3. ripgrep's --max-columns is the most cited best practice for search/summary tools.
4. Terminal-width-aware auto-truncation (bat style) is elegant for TTY, but
   lessence output is often piped, where width detection fails.

## Recommendation
Remove the hardcoded 200-char truncation from --summary. Instead:
- No truncation by default (matches ripgrep, git, kubectl)
- Future: add --max-columns N flag if users request it
- The agent that reported 103KB output should use --top N, not ask for truncation

## Sources
- ripgrep GUIDE.md: --max-columns=150 recommended config
- ripgrep issue #522: discussion on defaulting max-columns
- ag manpage: --width NUM flag
- bat manpage: --wrap auto, --chop-long-lines, --terminal-width
- lnav formats docs: max_width + overflow algorithms
- docker/cli container.go source: Ellipsis(command, 20)
- kubectl issue #7843: "laughably wide" output complaint
- git-log docs: --oneline shows full first line
close_reason: Research complete. Recommendation: remove hardcoded 200-char truncation, no truncation by default.

title: Wire up --disable-patterns for all accepted names (fix silent no-ops)
description:
`--disable-patterns` has three conflicting sources of truth, causing silent
no-ops for 8 of the 16 names it accepts today.

## The inconsistency

| Source | Location | Names |
|---|---|---|
| `--help` text | `main.rs:112` | 7 (missing `process`) |
| `VALID_PATTERNS` validator | `main.rs:27-44` | 16 |
| Actual wiring → `config.normalize_*` | `main.rs:218-229` | 8 |

User impact: `lessence --disable-patterns brackets` passes validation, prints
nothing, does nothing. Fails silently — matches the "must kill mutants, not
just not-panic" quality bar.

## Current behavior breakdown

**Tier A — honored (8):** timestamp, hash, network, uuid, process, email, path, duration.

**Tier B — validator accepts, hardcoded-on, silent no-op (2):** json, kubernetes.
See `main.rs:227, 229` (`normalize_json: true, // Always enabled for modern
log formats` / `normalize_kubernetes: true, // Always enabled for cloud-native
logs`). Deliberate design — must either (a) remove from VALID_PATTERNS or
(b) actually gate them. Author call.

**Tier C — validator accepts, no config wiring exists, silent no-op (6):**
http-status, brackets, key-value, quoted-string, name, decimal. These detectors
run unconditionally in `normalize.rs:115-175`.

**Tier D — detectors that run but aren't even in VALID_PATTERNS (2):**
log_module, structured. `normalize.rs:141, 149` — no way to disable at all.

## Work items

1. **Add config fields** in `config.rs`: `normalize_http_status`, `normalize_brackets`,
   `normalize_key_value`, `normalize_quoted`, `normalize_names`, `normalize_log_module`,
   `normalize_structured`. Default all to `true`.

2. **Wire the mapping** in `main.rs:218+`: map each validated name to its
   `config.normalize_*` field. Rename `brackets` → `bracket_context`,
   `quoted-string` → `quoted` in the VALID_PATTERNS list? (keep user-facing
   names stable, map internally.) Also add `log-module` and `structured` to
   VALID_PATTERNS.

3. **Gate the detectors** in `normalize.rs`: wrap each `detect_and_replace`
   call at lines 115, 123, 133, 141, 149, 167, 175 with a
   `if self.config.normalize_X { ... }` guard. Mirror the pattern already
   used for timestamps, paths, etc.

4. **Decide json/kubernetes:** either (a) drop from VALID_PATTERNS and update
   docs to say they're always-on by design; or (b) add real gating. Author
   decision at implementation time — mention the trade-off in the commit.

5. **Delete `decimal` from VALID_PATTERNS.** No `patterns/decimal.rs` exists.
   Decimal numbers are detected inside `DurationDetector`
   (`patterns/duration.rs:123-126`) — there is no meaningful way to extract
   a separate decimal-only disable without changing tokenization. Users who
   want to disable decimals already have `--disable-patterns duration`.
   (This step is required regardless of Option A vs B.)

6. **Sync the help text** at `main.rs:112`. It currently lists 7 names;
   after this work it should list the real set.

7. **Update docs:** `CLAUDE.md` ("Valid pattern names (16)") and the
   `lessence` skill at `.claude/skills/lessence/references/flags.md` must
   match the new set.

8. **Add tests** per name — at least one test per disable flag that confirms
   the detector's token type does NOT appear in the normalized output when
   disabled. These are low-cost snapshot-style assertions that actually
   exercise the wiring; cargo-mutants will catch regressions.

## Out of scope

- Refactoring normalize.rs detector dispatch into a table-driven loop. That's
  a separate cleanup and would make step 3 cleaner, but it's a bigger change.
- Exposing `--enable-only X,Y` as the inverse. Nice to have, not needed here.

## Acceptance

- `lessence --disable-patterns <any VALID_PATTERNS name>` either honors
  the flag (token type disappears) or is rejected by the validator.
- `--help` output, VALID_PATTERNS, and actual config wiring all list the
  same names.
- CLAUDE.md and lessence skill flags.md match.
- `cargo test` passes with per-name disable assertions.
close_reason: Resolved by fe1ccf2 — all 7 silent-no-op names are now wired and guarded, with per-flag tests killing the 'forgot to wire this' mutant class. decimal handled separately in 8ef9666 (removed from VALID_PATTERNS, no backing code existed).

comments.author: jorgen
comments.text: `decimal` handled separately in 8ef9666 — removed from VALID_PATTERNS with a `fix:` commit that lands in release notes explaining the history (never implemented; use `duration` which owns all numeric tokens). Bead scope now covers only the remaining 7 silent no-ops: brackets, key-value, http-status, quoted-string, name (Tier C, need config wiring), plus json, kubernetes (Tier B, deliberately always-on — decide: drop from VALID_PATTERNS or add gating).

title: Add -q/--quiet alias for --no-stats
description:
Add --quiet / -q as Unix-conventional alias for --no-stats.

1. Add short alias -q and long alias --quiet to existing no_stats field
2. Keep --no-stats working for backwards compatibility
3. Standard Unix convention (grep -q, curl -q, rsync -q)

Scope: one line change in clap derive attributes.
Acceptance: lessence -q suppresses stats footer.

title: Unit tests: format_group and output dispatch
description:
Add unit tests for group formatting functions in src/folder.rs.

1. format_group (66 mutants): main text/markdown formatter. Takes PatternGroup + GroupRollup, returns formatted string. Test collapsed vs non-collapsed, essence mode, compact marker presence.
2. format_group_json (22 mutants): JSON serialization of a group record. Test all fields present (id, count, normalized, token_types, first, last, time_range, variation). Test with/without timestamps.
3. format_group_dispatch (2 mutants): routes to text or JSON based on config. Test both paths.
4. finish_summary (76 mutants): renders summary mode output (one line per pattern). Test with various group counts, --top N interaction.
5. finish_top_n (20 mutants): renders top N output sorted by count.

Build PatternGroups with make_group helper already in tests.

title: Unit tests: pattern detectors (k8s, kv, structured, bracket)
description:
Add unit tests for newer pattern detector functions with high mutant counts.

1. KeyValueDetector::classify_value_type (52 mutants): classifies KV pair value types. Test string, number, bool, path, duration.
2. KeyValueDetector::has_key_value_indicators (30 mutants): checks for KV patterns. Test key=value, key:value, false positives.
3. KeyValueDetector::is_valid_key_value_context (28 mutants): validates KV context.
4. KeyValueDetector::is_common_config_pattern (24 mutants): identifies config patterns.
5. StructuredMessageDetector::has_kubernetes_indicators (48 mutants): checks for k8s patterns.
6. StructuredMessageDetector::has_structured_indicators (30 mutants): checks structured log indicators.
7. StructuredMessageDetector::is_valid_structured_log (34 mutants): validates structured log format.
8. StructuredMessageDetector::is_microservice_component (26 mutants): identifies microservice components.
9. LogWithModuleDetector::has_kubernetes_indicators (36 mutants): k8s module detection.
10. LogWithModuleDetector::has_log_module_indicators (34 mutants): log module detection.
11. BracketContextDetector::has_kubernetes_indicators (36 mutants): k8s bracket detection.
12. BracketContextDetector::has_bracket_indicators (30 mutants): bracket context detection.

Tests go in each pattern module's cfg(test) section.

title: JSON stats on stderr (--stats-json)
description:
Emit structured JSON statistics on stderr while compressed output goes to stdout.

1. New flag: --stats-json emits JSON to stderr after stdout is flushed
2. Fields: input_lines, output_lines, compression_ratio, groups, elapsed_ms, pattern_hits
3. Orthogonal to --quiet: --quiet --stats-json gives clean stdout + JSON stderr
4. Single JSON object at end (not NDJSON streaming)
5. Use serde_json::to_writer(stderr.lock(), &stats)

Prior art: ripgrep --stats prints to stderr. pv writes throughput to stderr.
Future: TTY auto-detection could auto-switch format (std::io::IsTerminal, Rust 1.70+).

Acceptance: lessence --stats-json emits valid JSON on stderr with correct counts.

title: Unit tests: pipeline and buffer management
description:
Add unit tests for pipeline functions in src/folder.rs.

1. apply_pii_masking (12 mutants): public function, replaces email tokens with masked versions. Test with/without emails, mixed tokens, empty tokens.
2. first_timestamp_in (8 mutants): extracts first Token::Timestamp from a token slice. Test with no timestamps, one timestamp, multiple timestamps, non-timestamp tokens only.
3. flush_oldest_safe_group (24 mutants): removes oldest group from buffer, formats it. Test buffer state before/after, returned string.
4. should_flush_buffer (6 mutants): returns true when buffer exceeds 1000 groups. Test at 999, 1000, 1001.
5. apply_second_similarity_pass (54 mutants): re-clusters buffer groups. Test that similar groups merge.
6. process_line (14 mutants): main hot path. Test single line processing, buffer growth.

Private functions, tests in cfg(test) mod tests.

title: Shell completions (--completions)
description:
Add shell completion generation using clap_complete.

1. Add clap_complete = "4" to dependencies
2. Add --completions <shell> flag (keeps lessence subcommand-free)
3. Supports bash, zsh, fish, elvish, powershell via Shell enum
4. Runtime generation (no build.rs needed)
5. Usage: lessence --completions bash > ~/.local/share/bash-completion/completions/lessence

Scope: ~15 lines of code + one dependency.
Acceptance: lessence --completions bash produces valid bash completion script.

title: Performance profiling: grouping pipeline optimization
description:
1. Profiled lessence pipeline on 50k synthetic log lines using hyperfine
2. Identified 3 slowest components via isolation benchmarks
3. Ran 14-iteration autoresearch optimize loop, 5 kept / 9 discarded
4. Achieved 45% parallel speedup (1294ms to 712ms) and 54% worst-case improvement (8565ms to 3937ms)
5. Net code reduction of 14 lines across folder.rs and normalize.rs
design:
## Bottom 3 Performance Bottlenecks (measured)

### #1: Linear-scan grouping with Vec<char> allocation (FIXED)
The similarity_score() method allocated two Vec<char> per comparison call.
With 832 unique groups (worst case), that was 41.6M heap allocations across
50k lines. O(n*k) where k = unique groups.

Fix: replaced with direct &[u8] byte comparison. Zero allocation.
Result: worst-case 8565ms to 3880ms (55% faster).

### #2: Non-disableable always-on detectors (PARTIALLY FIXED)
7 pattern detectors run unconditionally on every line (HttpStatus, Brackets,
KeyValue, LogModule, Structured, Name, QuotedString). Each allocates a String
even when no pattern is found.

Fix: added cheap contains() pre-filters (skip detector if line has no '[',
'=', '{', or quotes). Removed dead if-true guards.
Result: ~100ms improvement on stress test. Full fix would require Cow<str>
return type across all 17 detectors (future work).

### #3: Rayon batch size too small (FIXED)
Default batch of 100 lines meant 500 rayon synchronization points per 50k
lines. Each sync has overhead (thread pool wake, work stealing, barrier).

Fix: increased to 10,000 lines per batch (5 syncs per 50k lines).
Result: parallel 1294ms to 712ms (45% faster).

## Experiment Log

| # | Hypothesis | Result | Status |
|---|-----------|--------|--------|
| 1 | HashMap for O(1) exact match | No gain, fuzzy path dominates | discarded |
| 2 | Byte-level similarity (no Vec<char>) | 55% faster worst-case | KEPT |
| 3 | Integer-only length rejection | Within noise | discarded |
| 4 | HashMap + byte sim combined | Hash overhead eats gains | discarded |
| 5 | Move-to-front heuristic | No gain on uniform data | discarded |
| 6 | Vec pre-alloc + dedup branch | -10 lines, equal perf | KEPT (simpler) |
| 7 | Lower flush threshold (1000 to 100) | Compression 74.8% to 56.7% | discarded (regression) |
| 8 | Pre-filter always-on detectors | ~100ms gain, cleaner code | KEPT |
| 9 | Skip initial String clone | Within noise, adds complexity | discarded |
| 10 | Threshold-adaptive length rejection | Within noise | discarded |
| 11 | Batch 100 to 1000 | Parallel 1294 to 808ms | KEPT |
| 12 | Batch 1000 to 5000 | Parallel 808 to 738ms | KEPT |
| 13 | Batch 5000 to 10000 | Parallel 738 to 712ms | KEPT |
| 14 | Batch 10000 to 50000 | Plateau, worse memory | discarded |

## Key Insight
The paradox: disabling all pattern detectors made the tool 2x SLOWER.
Better normalization = fewer unique groups = less similarity work.
Performance is dominated by the interaction between normalization quality
and grouping cost, not by any single component.

## Remaining Opportunities
1. Cow<str> return type for detect_and_replace (avoid 800k String allocs)
2. Hash-bucketed grouping (partition groups by normalized hash prefix)
3. SIMD byte comparison in similarity_score (AVX2 on x86_64)
4. Profile-guided optimization (PGO) for 10-20% overall improvement
close_reason: Research complete, 4 optimizations merged to main via perf commit

title: Guard apply_pii_masking loop against infinite loops
description:
src/folder.rs:37-42 — the while loop in apply_pii_masking advances
start by abs_pos + email.len(). If email is empty or the arithmetic
is wrong, start never advances and the loop hangs forever.

Found by cargo-mutants: replacing + with - on line 41 creates an
infinite loop that hangs for 69s until timeout kills it.

Fix: add two guards:
1. Skip empty email tokens (continue)
2. Check next_start > start before advancing (break if not)

This is a defensive code fix, not a test fix.

title: CI exit codes (--fail-on-pattern)
description:
Exit non-zero when a regex pattern matches input lines. For CI pipeline gating.

1. --fail-on-pattern "ERROR|FATAL" takes a regex
2. Exit codes: 0=clean, 1=pattern matched, 2=tool error (trivy/hadolint convention)
3. Always emit compressed output AND exit non-zero (diagnosis + gating)
4. Scan raw lines during ingestion, set flag, check after output completes
5. CI usage: lessence --fail-on-pattern ERROR < build.log || alert

Start with regex only. Semantic --fail-on-level deferred until level awareness exists.
Acceptance: exits 1 when input contains matching lines, 0 when it does not.
close_reason: Added --fail-on-pattern for CI exit code gating
